summary.en: Traditionally a mobile telecom operator did three businesses under one roof — running the network (infrastructure management), acquiring and retaining subscribers (customer relationship), and inventing new mobile products (product innovation). Each obeys a different economic logic: scale, scope, speed. Carriers like France Telecom, KPN, Vodafone, and Bharti Airtel are unbundling: outsourcing the network to equipment manufacturers (Nokia Siemens, Ericsson, Alcatel-Lucent), licensing innovation from creative shops (Mobilizy, tat), and keeping only the relationship business in-house. Drawn from Osterwalder & Pigneur, Business Model Generation pp. 62-63.
summary.zh: 传统移动通信运营商把三种生意揉在一个屋檐下——运营网络(基础设施管理)、获取和留存用户(客户关系)、发明新的移动产品(产品创新)。三者经济逻辑不同:规模、范围、速度。法国电信、KPN、Vodafone、印度 Bharti Airtel 等运营商正在解绑:把网络外包给设备制造商(诺基亚西门子、爱立信、阿尔卡特朗讯),把创新外包给小型创意公司(Mobilizy、tat),自己只保留客户关系业务。素材来自 Osterwalder & Pigneur《商业模式新生代》第 62-63 页。

sources.label: Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Unbundling the Mobile Telco (pp. 62-63)
Hagel III & Singer · Unbundling the Corporation · HBR 1999
